step2_verification.qa_pairs:
1. [SUPPORTED] Q: Does the document describe "CVE-2023-34362" as being actively exploited by the threat actor(s) covered in this report (not merely mentioned as background context)?
   A: Yes.
2. [SUPPORTED] Q: Does the document describe "CVE-2022-21587" as being actively exploited by the threat actor(s) covered in this report (not merely mentioned as background context)?
   A: Yes.
3. [SUPPORTED] Q: Does the document describe "CVE-2023-2868" as being actively exploited by the threat actor(s) covered in this report (not merely mentioned as background context)?
   A: Yes.
4. [SUPPORTED] Q: Does the document describe "BEACON" as a tool, malware, or utility used BY the threat actor(s) in this report (not a defensive or victim tool)?
   A: Yes, the document describes "BEACON" as a backdoor malware written in C/C++ that is part of the Cobalt Strike framework and is used by threat actors to conduct operations.
5. [SUPPORTED] Q: Does the document describe "ALPHV" as a tool, malware, or utility used BY the threat actor(s) in this report (not a defensive or victim tool)?
   A: Yes, the document describes "ALPHV" as a ransomware written in Rust used BY threat actors.
6. [SUPPORTED] Q: Does the document describe "LEMURLOOT" as a tool, malware, or utility used BY the threat actor(s) in this report (not a defensive or victim tool)?
   A: Yes, the document describes "LEMURLOOT" as a web shell written in C# tailored to interact with the MOVEit Transfer platform and used by attackers following exploitation of the MOVEit technology.
7. [NOT SUPPORTED] Q: Does the document describe the technique "T1059" (Command and Scripting Interpreter) as being used specifically for "Execution" — not a different tactic or phase of the attack?
   A: No.
8. [NOT SUPPORTED] Q: Does the document describe the technique "T1027" (Obfuscated Files or Information) as being used specifically for "Defense Evasion" — not a different tactic or phase of the attack?
   A: No.
9. [NOT SUPPORTED] Q: Does the document describe the technique "T1190" (Exploit Public-Facing Application) as being used specifically for "Initial Access" — not a different tactic or phase of the attack?
   A: No.
10. [SUPPORTED] Q: Does the document describe the technique "T1566" (Phishing) as being used specifically for "Initial Access" — not a different tactic or phase of the attack?
   A: Yes.

item.evidence_snippet: Top ATT&CK techniques observed: T1059 Command and Scripting Interpreter, T1027 Obfuscated Files or Information 46.5%.
T1027 Obfuscated Files or Information 46.5% was among the top ATT&CK techniques observed in Mandiant investigations.
T1190 Exploit Public-Facing Application 28.7% was observed as a top initial access technique in Mandiant investigations.
